message.content:
You are an INDEPENDENT verifier adjudicating audit finding GitHub issue #14 for /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. You did NOT author it. TRUE_POSITIVE vs FALSE_POSITIVE; try HARD to refute. READ-ONLY: no edits, no git, no gh. Use Read/grep and /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.

FINDING (#14, stated severity LOW): src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py — inconsistent stub honesty: every other infra seam is a bare Protocol or fails loud (UnconfiguredReasoningSeam raises NotImplementedError), but NoopTracer is a silent no-op named like a drop-in default, while its module header claims "loopback enforcement and credential scrubbing carry over" — behavior the no-op does not provide. Mitigating: NoopTracer is not in __all__ and not wired as a default anywhere.

Independently verify: read tracing.py; confirm NoopTracer.span just yields; confirm it's absent from __all__ and not referenced/defaulted anywhere (grep NoopTracer in src/). Compare with reasoning/seam.py's fail-loud stub. Decide TRUE/PARTIAL/FALSE. Assess LOW severity.

Return EXACTLY:
VERDICT: TRUE_POSITIVE | FALSE_POSITIVE | PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: high | medium | low
SEVERITY_ASSESSMENT: <...>
REASONING: <your own evidence>
FIX_GUIDANCE: <...>

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Self-hosted tracing seam (contract + stub).
2	
3	OpenTelemetry → on-box collector. The engine emits spans through this seam so the concrete
4	exporter (loopback-bound local collector) can be configured once and enforced everywhere.
5	Lifted from the prior platform; loopback enforcement and credential scrubbing carry over.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from contextlib import contextmanager
11	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, runtime_checkable
12	
13	
14	@runtime_checkable
15	class Tracer(Protocol):
16	    @contextmanager
17	    def span(self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> Iterator[None]:
18	        ...
19	
20	
21	class NoopTracer:
22	    """A tracer that records nothing — default until the local collector is wired."""
23	
24	    @contextmanager
25	    def span(self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> Iterator[None]:
26	        yield
27

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam (contract + stub).
2	
3	Implementation note: lifted from the prior platform's tiered reasoning_client —
4	Tier 2 (local runtime, bulk) and Tier 3 (frontier model via fixed-cost subscription OAuth,
5	high-stakes). Subscription credentials are scrubbed from traces/logs; no metered API key is
6	ever read. The concrete client is wired when the infra is ported.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol, runtime_checkable
14	
15	
16	class StakesTier(Enum):
17	    BULK = "bulk"          # local model — high volume, low stakes
18	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"   # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
19	
20	
21	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
22	class ReasoningRequest:
23	    prompt: str
24	    tier: StakesTier
25	    purpose: str
26	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
27	
28	
29	@runtime_checkable
30	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
31	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
32	
33	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
34	        ...
35	
36	
37	class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam:
38	    """Placeholder until the tiered client is ported. Fails loudly rather than silently."""
39	
40	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:  # noqa: D401
41	        raise NotImplementedError(
42	            "reasoning seam not yet wired — port the tiered reasoning_client from the "
43	            "hardened platform (local bulk tier + subscription high-stakes tier, ADR-0003)"
44	        )
45

content.text: The `tracing.py` file itself has no `__all__`. Let me check the package `__init__.py` to confirm `NoopTracer` is not exported, and check how other infra seams are structured for the consistency comparison.

1	"""Reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly. The seam routes by *stakes*: a local model for
4	bulk/low-stakes work, a frontier model via a fixed-cost subscription path for high-stakes
5	work (no metered API — ADR-0003). It owns timeouts, retries, and tracing.
6	
7	To be lifted and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client``.
8	"""

10	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import ReasoningRequest, ReasoningSeam, StakesTier

content.text:
I now have all the evidence needed.

VERDICT: TRUE_POSITIVE
CONFIDENCE: high

SEVERITY_ASSESSMENT: LOW is accurate. The mitigating factors are real and substantial: `NoopTracer` is never exported (not in `tracing.py` — which has no `__all__` — nor in `observability/__init__.py`'s `__all__`, which lists only `Tracer` and `ExperimentTracker`), and it is never instantiated, imported, or wired as a default anywhere in `src/` or `tests/`. The only occurrence is its definition. So there is currently no live consumer that would silently swallow spans. The finding is a code-honesty/consistency issue (latent footgun + misleading docstring), not an active correctness or security defect. LOW is appropriate; arguably even INFO/nit given total absence of wiring.

REASONING (independent evidence):
- `NoopTracer.span` (tracing.py:24-26) is a `@contextmanager` whose body is a bare `yield` — confirmed it records nothing.
- `__all__` claim verified: `tracing.py` defines no `__all__`; `observability/__init__.py:11` exports only `["Tracer", "ExperimentTracker"]`. `NoopTracer` is omitted. It is not re-exported anywhere.
- Grep for `NoopTracer` across `src/` and `tests/` yields exactly one hit: the class definition (tracing.py:21). No instantiation, no default assignment, no DI registration. The "default until the local collector is wired" claim in its own docstring (tracing.py:22) is currently aspirational — nothing actually defaults to it.
- Contrast with `reasoning/seam.py:37-44`: `UnconfiguredReasoningSeam.run` raises `NotImplementedError` with an explicit "Fails loudly rather than silently" docstring. The sibling `ExperimentTracker` (tracking.py) is a bare Protocol with no stub impl at all. So the module-level pattern is indeed: fail-loud stub OR bare Protocol — and `NoopTracer` is the lone silent no-op, confirming the inconsistency claim.
- The misleading-header claim is also valid: `tracing.py:5` states "loopback enforcement and credential scrubbing carry over," yet `NoopTracer` provides neither (it does nothing). A reader who later wires `NoopTracer` as a default — encouraged by both its name and its own docstring — would silently get zero spans, zero loopback enforcement, and zero scrubbing, with no error.

Refutation attempt: I tried to refute by checking whether `NoopTracer` is actually wired as a default (which would raise severity) or whether it's referenced in config/factory code — it is not, anywhere. I also checked whether it might be exported transitively — it is not. Both checks support the finding's own mitigating note rather than overturning the finding. The core claims (silent no-op, not in `__all__`, not defaulted, inconsistent with fail-loud sibling, header overpromises) all hold exactly as stated.

FIX_GUIDANCE: Two clean options.
1. Preferred — make it fail loud like the reasoning seam: delete `NoopTracer` and either rely on the bare `Tracer` Protocol (matching `ExperimentTracker`'s pattern), or replace it with an `UnconfiguredTracer` whose `span` raises `NotImplementedError` referencing ADR-0003 / the unwired local collector. This restores module-wide consistency.
2. If a genuine no-op is wanted for tests, rename it to make the silence explicit (e.g. keep `NoopTracer` but add a short comment that it is a test double, not a production default), remove the "default until ... wired" wording from its docstring, and scope the misleading header line so loopback/scrubbing promises don't appear to apply to the no-op.

In all cases, correct the `tracing.py:5` header so it does not attribute loopback enforcement and credential scrubbing to a tracer that performs neither, and avoid naming any stub like a drop-in production default unless it is one.
